title: 1153 West Fayette AV Equipment Budget Amendment
summary: Michael Layman, Deputy Commissioner of Assessment, presents item 33: amending a prior ordinance to increase Huber Brewer's authorization for audiovisual equipment purchase and installation at 1153 West Fayette. No additional money requested -- reallocating within the $2M FFE bond authorized last summer. Council questions whether items 26 and 33 would be combined (separate accounts, different departments).
raw_content:
[00:54:51] item 33.
[00:54:58] >> Good afternoon, counselors. Michael
[00:54:59] Layman, deputy commissioner with the
[00:55:00] assessment department. Uh just want to
[00:55:02] preface this by saying we aren't asking
[00:55:04] for any additional money. This uh is
[00:55:06] asking to amend an ordinance that had
[00:55:08] authorized funds to be paid to Huber
[00:55:10] Brewer for the purchase and installation
[00:55:13] of audiovisisual equipment. We're just
[00:55:15] looking to increase that amount. And
[00:55:17] this is that increase is based on bonds
[00:55:20] that were authorized last summer. So
[00:55:22] really just moving numbers from one
[00:55:23] column to another. No additional funding
[00:55:26] is being requested today.
[00:55:30] >> So is is that that at the end of it is
[00:55:33] going to combine 26 and 33 together
[00:55:35] eventually. Is that the idea?
[00:55:38] >> Can you say that again please?
processing_notes: 1153 West Fayette is the new joint public safety headquarters. Assessment department managing FFE procurement. Huber Brewer is the AV vendor. Detail continues in segment 14.
Segment 13 of 14 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 4th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
